policy_rules.policy_text: Minimum Current Ratio of 1.20 shall be maintained at the time of sanction and throughout the facility tenure. For seasonal industries (textiles, food processing), a minimum of 1.10 may be accepted during off-season with proper justification.
Maximum Debt-Equity Ratio of 2.00 is permitted. For infrastructure/capital-intensive industries, up to 3.00 may be considered with Credit Committee approval.

policy_rules.formula: Total Outside Liabilities / Tangible Net Worth
policy_rules.policy_text: Total Outside Liabilities to Tangible Net Worth ratio shall not exceed 3.00 for manufacturing units and 4.00 for trading units. TOL includes all liabilities other than net worth.

Minimum Quick Ratio of 1.00 shall be maintained. This ratio measures the ability to meet short-term obligations without relying on inventory sales. For manufacturing units with fast-moving inventory, relaxation up to 0.80 may be considered.
Minimum Cash Ratio of 0.20 is recommended to ensure adequate liquid reserves for immediate obligations. Lower ratios may be acceptable if strong unutilized banking limits are available.
Minimum Debt Service Coverage Ratio of 1.25 shall be maintained for Term Loan facilities. For project finance, average DSCR over the loan tenure should be minimum 1.50 with no year below 1.10.

Minimum Interest Coverage Ratio of 2.00 is required. ICR = EBIT / Interest Expense. For units with high depreciation, EBITDA-based coverage may also be considered. ICR below 1.50 is a red flag requiring detailed analysis.
Minimum Gross Profit Margin of 10% is required. For trading businesses, minimum 5% is acceptable. Industry benchmarks should be considered for sector-specific variations.
Minimum EBITDA Margin of 8% indicates operational efficiency. For service industries, minimum 12% is expected. Declining margins over 3 consecutive years should be flagged for review.
Minimum Net Profit Margin (PAT Margin) of 2% is required. Loss-making units may be considered only if EBITDA is positive and there is a clear path to profitability within 2 years.
Minimum Return on Equity of 15% indicates efficient use of shareholders funds. ROE below 10% for 2 consecutive years requires enhanced monitoring.
Minimum Return on Assets of 5% demonstrates effective asset utilization. Capital-intensive industries may have lower ROA benchmarks.
